title: sambucus pubescens var. heptaphylla
intro: Sambucus pubescens var. heptaphylla, also known as the American elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, containing flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of American elderberry include reducing symptoms of cold and flu, alleviating arthritis and joint pain, and supporting cardiovascular health, thanks to its rich antioxidant content. American elderberry is traditionally prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and can also be consumed as a supplement or used topically in salves and creams.
